title: [MemCpyOpt] support offset slices for performStackMoveOptzn and processMemCpy
author: vtjnash
description:
In particular, support offset of src, since offset of dest will be a followup change when dest is allowed to be not full-sized with copy.

Extracted from https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/150792

tests.test_body:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i64(ptr noalias nocapture writeonly, ptr noalias nocapture readonly, i64, i1 immarg) #0

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr immarg) #1

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr immarg) #1

declare void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture)

define void @memcpy_src_gep_to_dest_alloca() {
  %src = alloca [16 x i8], align 4
  %dest = alloca [8 x i8], align 8
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %src)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %dest)
  %src.gep = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %src, i64 8
  store i64 42, ptr %src.gep, align 4
  call void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture %src.gep)
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i64(ptr align 8 %dest, ptr align 8 %src.gep, i64 8, i1 false)
  call void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture %dest)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %src)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %dest)
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite) }
attributes #1 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite) }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr immarg) #1

declare void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture)

define void @no_optimize_dest_has_offset() {
  %src = alloca [16 x i8], align 4
  %dest = alloca [16 x i8], align 8
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %src)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %dest)
  %src.gep = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %src, i64 8
  %dest.gep = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %dest, i64 8
  store i64 42, ptr %src.gep, align 4
  call void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture %src.gep)
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i64(ptr align 4 %dest.gep, ptr align 4 %src.gep, i64 8, i1 false)
  call void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture %dest.gep)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %src)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %dest)
  ret void
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr immarg) #0

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr immarg) #0

declare void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture)

define void @load_store_src_gep_to_dest_alloca() {
  %src = alloca [8 x i8], align 4
  %dest = alloca [4 x i8], align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %src)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %dest)
  %src.gep = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %src, i64 4
  store i32 42, ptr %src.gep, align 4
  call void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture %src.gep)
  %val = load i32, ptr %src.gep, align 4
  store i32 %val, ptr %dest, align 4
  call void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture %dest)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %src)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %dest)
  ret void
}

define void @memcpy_src_gep_offset4_to_dest_alloca() {
  %src = alloca [12 x i8], align 4
  %dest = alloca [8 x i8], align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %src)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %dest)
  %src.gep = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %src, i64 4
  store i64 42, ptr %src.gep, align 4
  call void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture %src.gep)
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i64(ptr align 4 %dest, ptr align 4 %src.gep, i64 8, i1 false)
  call void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture %dest)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %src)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %dest)
  ret void
}

define void @memcpy_both_direct_allocas() {
  %src = alloca [8 x i8], align 4
  %dest = alloca [8 x i8], align 8
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %src)
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %dest)
  store i64 42, ptr %src, align 4
  call void @use_nocapture(ptr nocapture %src)

comments.author: chapuni
comments.body: Seems this broke selfhosting (in my private builders). Investigating.

comments.author: aeubanks
comments.body: I also see a miscompile with this PR, reproducible on x86-64 linux with a stage 2 build of LLVM, running `build/stage2/bin/llvm-mca llvm/test/tools/llvm-mca/X86/cpus.s -mtriple=x86_64-unknown-unknown -mcpu=x86-64 -resource-pressure=false -instruction-info=false`. I had to use the sysroot from https://commondatastorage.googleapis.com/chrome-linux-sysroot/dec7a3a0fc5b83b909cba1b6d119077e0429a138eadef6bf5a0f2e03b1904631 for some reason

comments.author: vtjnash
comments.body:
@aeubanks is there any other details you can provide about what was miscompiled about that command? downloading a random sysroot to reproduce feels like a bit large of a step to take. Is that by chance an older c++ stdlib? (the other report of miscompile also only occurred with old stdlibs)

EDIT: I see that is Debian Bullseye, circa 2021-2024
